summary:
Prolactin secretion is primarily regulated by inhibition from Dopamine produced within the Hypothalamus; Growth Hormone does not have such an identified direct inhibitory control from the same region based on current knowledge.
The primary inhibitor of prolactin release originates from the Hypothalamus' release of Dopamine, while there isn't sufficient information suggesting similar regulatory mechanisms exist between other hormones listed here and their respective hypothalamic controls. The summary highlights how prolactin stands out due to its well-documented relationship with hypthahmalic suppression mediated largely through dopamine signaling pathways. Agent1 reasoned that amongst various hormonal systems discussed, prolactin alone has strong scientific backing showing it’s mainly controlled at the level of the Hypothalamus via dopamine action which acts oppositely i.e., inhibitively towards increasing levels of prolactin when present leading upregulation elsewhere would cause increased synthesis & subsequent release into bloodstream causing imbalance conditions like HyperProlactenemia if disrupted normally functioning feedback loop fails somehow disrupting natural homeostasis maintained otherwise efficiently without fail barring rare
